perturbation.original_text: "Customer" means any Person that is a customer of the Distributor and any Affiliate of Distributor that has an interest in or ultimately utilizes the Product (as defined below).
perturbation.changed_text: "Client" means any Person that is a customer of the Distributor and any Affiliate of Distributor that has an interest in or ultimately utilizes the Product (as defined below).
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Customer' has been changed to 'Client' in Section 1.1. This creates an in-text contradiction because the contract uses both 'Customer' and 'Client' without defining them as interchangeable, leading to uncertainty about which entities are being referred to in different contexts. This is particularly impactful in sections describing obligations relating to customers or clients.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Delivery; Storage; Risk of Loss. Hydraspin shall use commercially reasonable efforts to deliver Products to Customers by the delivery dates specified in Distributor's Orders. Products shall be delivered directly to the Customer. All costs and expenses of shipping, freight, delivery, transportation and storage, including, without limitation, insurance covering the Products during transportation and while stored at any facility will be borne solely by Hydraspin and the Distributor shall not be liable therefor. Risk of loss shall be borne entirely by Hydraspin, even while Products are in the possession or control of Distributor or any customer, and the Distributor and the customers shall not be liable therefor, except solely in the case of Distributor's or any customer's intentional misconduct or gross negligence. Hydraspin may request Distributor to store Products at Distributor's facilities, and Distributor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to accommodate Hydraspin's storage requests; provided, that Hydraspin shall bear the full risk of loss with respect to Products stored at Distributor's facility and Distributor shall not be liable therefor, except solely in the case of Distributor's intentional misconduct or gross negligence.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Customer" to "Client" creates a direct contradiction because the Contradicted Text explicitly uses the term "Customers" when discussing delivery and risk of loss. Since the contract no longer clearly defines who 'Customers' are, this section becomes ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. The 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition applies because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: "Products" shall mean, collectively, (i) the products listed on Exhibit A attached hereto, as such Exhibit may be amended from time to time by the Parties, (ii) all modifications, alterations, improvements, upgrades, and replacements to the products listed on Exhibit "A", now existing and existing in the future, and (iii) all other products, now existing and existing in the future, distributed by Hydraspin or any Affiliate thereof that perform substantially the same functions as the products listed on Exhibit "A", now existing and existing in the future.
perturbation.changed_text: "Goods" shall mean, collectively, (i) the products listed on Exhibit A attached hereto, as such Exhibit may be amended from time to time by the Parties, (ii) all modifications, alterations, improvements, upgrades, and replacements to the products listed on Exhibit "A", now existing and existing in the future, and (iii) all other products, now existing and existing in the future, distributed by Hydraspin or any Affiliate thereof that perform substantially the same functions as the products listed on Exhibit "A", now existing and existing in the future.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Products' to 'Goods' in the definition introduces ambiguity. The agreement consistently uses 'Products' throughout the text, specifically relating to Hydrocarbon separation technology. By redefining the term as 'Goods' in this section, it creates uncertainty if the term 'Products' used elsewhere refers to the same items, something different, or if the contract is now referring to a broader category of merchandise. This will lead to potential disputes over obligations related to product performance, intellectual property, and exclusivity, since the definition is specifically for Hydraspin separation equipment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exclusivity and Territory. Hydraspin hereby appoints Distributor, and Distributor hereby accepts appointment, as Hydraspin's exclusive distributor of the Products in the Territory during the term of this Agreement, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, including, but not limited to, the satisfaction of the Performance Benchmarks. The Territory may be amended with the mutual agreement of both Parties from time to time to add or delete geographic territories by amending Exhibit B attached hereto. If Hydraspin desires to enter a new territory in the United States, Hydraspin will offer Distributor the first opportunity to become the exclusive distributor for the new territory. If the Parties are unable to reach an agreement on the terms of exclusivity within ten (10) business days of the date the opportunity is presented to Distributor, Hydraspin shall have no obligation to enter into a contract with Distributor regarding the new territory. In the event the Distributor loses exclusivity on a territory due to not meeting Performance Benchmarks, the Distributor shall maintain exclusivity on any and all existing Products that are in the field and operating at them time exclusivity if forfeited.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Products' to 'Goods' introduces a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text, 'Exclusivity and Territory. Hydraspin hereby appoints Distributor, and Distributor hereby accepts appointment, as Hydraspin's exclusive distributor of the Products in the Territory...', clearly relies on a specific understanding of what 'Products' means. The original definition of 'Products' provided a clear scope related to Hydraspin's offerings. By changing this to 'Goods', the contract now lacks clarity on whether the exclusivity applies only to the originally defined 'Products' (Hydrocarbon separation technology as explained), or a potentially broader range of 'Goods'. This undermines the enforceability of the exclusivity clause, creating a significant and immediate contradiction. The original text made it clear what products Hydraspin was assigning territory rights to, and now the changed text leaves this point ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: Hydraspin shall provide the Products to Distributor's Customers at no cost to Distributor or the Customers, except solely for the Hydraspin Share of Net Revenue, which shall be due and payable to Hydraspin as set forth in this Article VI. Likewise, Hydraspin shall install, service and maintain the Products, with the commercially reasonable assistance of Distributor, at no cost to Distributor or the customers except solely for the Hydraspin Share of Net Revenue, which shall be due and payable to Hydraspin as set forth in this Article VI.
perturbation.changed_text: Hydraspin shall provide the Products to Distributor's Customers at no cost to Distributor or the Customers, except solely for the Hydraspin Remittance of Net Revenue, which shall be due and payable to Hydraspin as set forth in this Article VI. Likewise, Hydraspin shall install, service and maintain the Products, with the commercially reasonable assistance of Distributor, at no cost to Distributor or the customers except solely for the Hydraspin Remittance of Net Revenue, which shall be due and payable to Hydraspin as set forth in this Article VI.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'Hydraspin Share' has been replaced with 'Hydraspin Remittance' in Article VI Section 6.1. This change contradicts the already defined term 'Hydraspin Share' within Article I Section 1.1. The agreement no longer clearly defines 'Hydraspin Remittance,' creating confusion about how revenue is divided, which affects payment obligations. The ambiguity in revenue sharing may lead to financial disputes between the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Hydraspin Share" means, with respect to Net Revenue, the percentage of Net Revenue that Hydraspin is entitled to receive, as follows: (i) for the first ten (10) Products installed, 92.5% of Net Revenue, and (ii) for the eleventh (11th) Product installed and all Products installed thereafter, 85% of Net Revenue. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained herein, the Hydraspin Share with respect to the split of Net Revenue between Hydraspin and the Distributor with respect to any particular Production installation or group Product installation may be negotiated by Hydraspin and the Distributor and set forth in a separate written agreement between the Parties, and in such case, the Hydraspin Share set forth in the separate written agreement shall supersede and control over the Hydraspin Share set forth above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Hydraspin Share' with 'Hydraspin Remittance' in Article VI, while Article I Section 1.1 defines 'Hydraspin Share.' This creates a direct conflict as the contract now uses a term ('Hydraspin Remittance') without definition, contradicting the explicitly defined 'Hydraspin Share' and introducing ambiguity about revenue division, directly impacting payment obligations and creating an in-text contradiction according to the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology.'
